channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Cape Verde's historic World Cup ride ends after pushing Argentina to the brink
item.description: Cape Verde didn't win a match at the World Cup, and somehow, that didn't seem to matter. The African team's debut on this stage was unforgettable.
item.content:encoded:
Cape Verde didn't win a match at the World Cup, and somehow, that didn't seem to matter. The African team's debut on this stage was unforgettable.

(Image credit: Lynne Sladky)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Iran begins dayslong funeral for the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
item.description: Iran began a dayslong funeral Saturday for the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, months after an airstrike killed him at the start of the war. He was 86.
item.content:encoded:
Iran began a dayslong funeral Saturday for the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, months after an airstrike killed him at the start of the war. He was 86.

(Image credit: Altaf Qadri)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Iran's control of the Strait of Hormuz remains a powerful bargaining chip
item.description: Despite efforts by U.S. negotiators, Iran says it wants to charge a toll for ships to pass through the Strait of Hormuz. It's yet another unresolved issue of the U.S.-Iran war.
item.content:encoded: Despite efforts by U.S. negotiators, Iran says it wants to charge a toll for ships to pass through the Strait of Hormuz. It's yet another unresolved issue of the U.S.-Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: Iran plans dayslong funeral for Supreme Leader Khamenei after war death
item.description: The country's theocracy hopes to see millions flood the streets of the capital beginning Saturday in scenes reminiscent to the burial of the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini in 1989.
item.content:encoded:
The country's theocracy hopes to see millions flood the streets of the capital beginning Saturday in scenes reminiscent to the burial of the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini in 1989.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: July 4th events threatened by heat wave. And, Russia strikes on Ukraine's capital
item.description: July 4th events for America's milestone birthday are being threatened by a brutal heat wave. And, Russia has struck Ukraine's capital, killing several people in what it calls retaliatory attacks.
item.content:encoded:
July 4th events for America's milestone birthday are being threatened by a brutal heat wave. And, Russia has struck Ukraine's capital, killing several people in what it calls retaliatory attacks.

(Image credit: Mario Tama)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Australian officials ask fans to respect the privacy of Neil, a trouble-making seal
item.description: The 5-year-old seal has a social media following twice the size of Tasmania's population, and his antics include bending traffic bollards and blocking roads.
item.content:encoded:
The 5-year-old seal has a social media following twice the size of Tasmania's population, and his antics include bending traffic bollards and blocking roads.

(Image credit: Sam Volker Photography)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Ex-HK bookseller Lam Wing-kee, detained by China in 2015, dies in Taiwan at 70
item.description: Lam was one of five people from Causeway Bay Books who disappeared in 2015. He later detailed his detention by Chinese authorities.
item.content:encoded:
Lam was one of five people from Causeway Bay Books who disappeared in 2015. He later detailed his detention by Chinese authorities.

(Image credit: Kin Cheung)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Are the wars in Ukraine and Iran showing the limits of global powers?
item.description: We look at the Russian attacks on Kyiv and the overall state of the wars between Russia and Ukraine and the U.S. and Iran.
item.content:encoded: We look at the Russian attacks on Kyiv and the overall state of the wars between Russia and Ukraine and the U.S. and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly

item.title: Trump denies conflict of interest over crypto. And, Vatican excommunicates rebel group
item.description: Trump and his family earned over $1 billion last year through cryptocurrency ventures and other businesses. And, the Vatican declared that the traditionalist Society of St. Pius X has entered schism.
item.content:encoded:
Trump and his family earned over $1 billion last year through cryptocurrency ventures and other businesses. And, the Vatican declared that the traditionalist Society of St. Pius X has entered schism.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Former ethics lawyer says Trump's crypto poses 'clear conflict of interest'
item.description: Former White House ethics lawyer Richard Painter says President Trump "stands alone" in having substantial financial conflicts of interest and that, "for every other executive branch official, it would be a violation."
item.content:encoded:
Former White House ethics lawyer Richard Painter says President Trump "stands alone" in having substantial financial conflicts of interest and that, "for every other executive branch official, it would be a violation."

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: A Martínez
